behavior_summary: Provides AI/interaction logic for a peasant NPC: handles being looked at, used (player interaction), area enter/exit triggers, being targeted/cast upon, and runs two spawnable dialog/behavior routines.
U8P_PESANT2::leaveFastArea (Event 10) performs cleanup when the actor/Avatar leaves the NPC's fast-area trigger by clearing several stored reference slots via repeated resetRef calls.

enterFastArea (Event F) starts an ambient interaction routine when an actor enters the NPC's fast-area: selects a short routine (U8P_PESANT2::0468) or a longer routine (U8P_PESANT2::06CD) depending on map and NPC state.

use (Event 1) handles direct player interaction: sets info/exclude flags, clears some stored refs, issues a randomized greeting, spawns animation/method calls based on map and facing, and then launches finishing methods.

cast (Event 11) handles being targeted/attacked: loops waiting for a valid target, can spawn guard-related behavior (CALGUARD::0080) and produce randomized combat barks, and may clear the NPC's in-combat flag.

Spawned routines U8P_PESANT2::0468 and U8P_PESANT2::06CD implement randomized, stateful dialog loops with timing, selection of canned lines, checks against a global somebodyTalking flag, and spawn animation/facing updates.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: distinct handlers for look, use, enterFastArea, U8P_PESANT2::leaveFastArea, cast and two spawnable dialog loops — script primarily reacts to engine events.
enterFastArea makes a mapping decision (Npc::getMap() == 0x29) to choose between two distinct spawnable behaviors (0468 vs 06CD).
U8P_PESANT2::leaveFastArea performs linear cleanup: multiple resetRef(referenceId, pid) calls to clear stored references — deterministic cleanup path on area exit.

cast contains a short retry/wait loop using a local byte ([BP-02h]) and timed waits (FREE::1C19) to poll Npc::getTarget(); specific target values (e.g., 0 or 1) drive branching to guard/spawn behavior.

Spawned dialog routines loop with randomized delays (urandom / FREE::1C19), maintain a small local index/state ([BP-02h]) to choose lines, and gate speaking on a global somebodyTalking flag to avoid overlapping speech.
open_questions: Exact meanings and semantic roles of the cleared reference slots (IDs like 0x021C, 0x0205, 0x0204, 0x00F0, 0x0200) — they appear to be pointers/links the NPC stores, but to what game systems (quests, targets, followers) is not explicit.
The comparison with constant 0xFA present in enterFastArea/use: appears to be an early-out/state check (maybe 'disabled' or 'inactive' marker) but its precise semantic meaning is unknown.
Precise behaviors of FREE::1D62 and FREE::2A56 (used in dialog and ref-clearing): inferred from usage but their concrete implementations are not visible.

Meaning of target values returned from Npc::getTarget() (for example when equal to 1 cast spawns CALGUARD::0080): whether these are special sentinel targets (player, guard token) is inferred but uncertain.
What map id 0x29 corresponds to in-game (a special interior/area) and why it selects the shorter dialog routine.

main_function.name: U8P_PESANT2::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: Clean up per-NPC stored references and remove transient associations when the Avatar (or relevant actor) leaves the NPC's fast-area trigger region. This prevents retained references after the actor exits proximity.
entry_conditions: Event 10 (U8P_PESANT2::leaveFastArea) — triggered by leaving the NPC's fast-area trigger

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT2::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Area-exit cleanup handler: clears multiple stored reference slots using resetRef to forget associations when the actor leaves proximity.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT2::look
function_map.role: Respond to 'look' (examine) events: if dead bark 'dead peasant <pid>' else 'peasant <pid>'; minimal dialog output.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT2::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Area-entry handler: sets info/exclude, optionally returns early on a state check, and spawns a short or long dialog routine depending on map and NPC liveliness.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT2::cast
function_map.role: React to being targeted/cast upon: poll for a target, possibly call guard behavior, produce randomized combat/help barks and clear in-combat state in a branch.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT2::use
function_map.role: Handle player 'use' interaction: set states, clear some refs, issue randomized greeting lines, spawn animation/method calls based on map and facing, then finalize with another method.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT2::0468
function_map.role: Spawned short dialog/behavior loop: randomized timed barks, local state-driven line selection, and facing/animation updates.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT2::06CD
function_map.role: Spawned longer dialog/behavior loop: similar to 0468 but with extended lines/timing; used for alternate flavor of ambient interaction.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the short interaction/dialog routine when map condition is met and NPC is alive.
Spawn the longer interaction/dialog routine when map condition differs (map != 0x29) and NPC is alive.

external_calls.purpose: Clear stored reference slots for this NPC (resetRef(referenceId, pid)) as cleanup on area exit.
Emit a short text line to indicate the NPC identity or death state (e.g., 'peasant <pid>' or 'dead peasant <pid>').
Check whether NPC is dead and skip spawning interaction if dead.
Determine which dialog routine to spawn (0468 vs 06CD) based on map id.
Poll current combat/target id inside a wait loop to decide reaction or to call guard behavior.
Spawn guard-related behavior when a specific target condition is observed (inferred: request nearby guard help).
Clear in-combat state in a branch after spawning guard behavior or resolving the target condition.
Clear stored references or reset slots as part of preparing/handling direct player interaction.
Spawn an animation/action method as part of the 'use' interaction flow.
Spawn a facing/direction-specific method determined by Npc::getDir() to adjust NPC orientation/action during interaction.
Spawn a finishing method at the end of the use/interaction sequence.
Introduce timed delays between dialog iterations and manage pacing of the short interaction loop.
Introduce timed delays between dialog iterations and manage pacing of the longer interaction loop.
Emit one of several canned short lines chosen by randomized local state during the short interaction loop.
Emit one of several canned lines (longer flavor) chosen by randomized local state during the long interaction loop.
Spawn methods to adjust facing/animation as part of dialog line delivery.
Spawn methods to adjust facing/animation as part of dialog line delivery.
Use randomization to choose timing and dialog variants for the spawned interaction.

reads.name: somebodyTalking
reads.likely_effect: Used to avoid overlapping dialog: spawn routines check this flag before deciding to speak or modify dialog index behavior.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides AI/interaction logic for a peasant NPC: handles being looked at, used (player interaction), area enter/exit triggers, being targeted/cast upon, and runs two spawnable dialog/behavior routines.
